title: Getting started with edr4r
text: client just stores connection settings (base URL, user agent, timeout, retry policy). Pass verbose = TRUE echo every request URL, headers = attach auth tokens.
code: library(edr4r)  client <- edr_client("https://api.wwdh.internetofwater.app") client

title: Getting started with edr4r
text: edr_collections() lists every EDR collection service serves. data_queries column tells EDR query types collection supports (locations, cube, area, …). Hit verb server doesn’t implement get HTTP error. see data parameters collection exposes (values can pass parameter_name = query verbs), use edr_parameters(): edr_queryables() something different – returns OGC queryables JSON Schema (filterable feature properties CQL2 / OGC API Features). discovering parameter names, edr_parameters() want.
code: collections <- edr_collections(client) collections[, c("id", "title", "data_queries")] params <- edr_parameters(client, "rise-edr") params #> # A tibble: 782 × 6 #>   id    name                          description unit_symbol unit_label #>   <chr> <chr>                         <chr>       <chr>       <chr> #> 1 1835  Secondary Canal Stage         "Average …" ft          Secondary… #> 2 1834  Lake/Reservoir Elevation      "Average …" ft          Lake/Rese… #> 3 1830  Lake/Reservoir Release Rate   "Instant…"  cfs         Lake/Rese… #> ... # Pick out the daily reservoir storage parameter (id "3"): params[params$id == "3", ] #> # A tibble: 1 × 6 #>   id    name                              description unit_symbol unit_label #>   <chr> <chr>                             <chr>       <chr>       <chr> #> 1 3     Daily Lake/Reservoir Storage      RISE Param… acre·ft     Acre Foot

title: Getting started with edr4r
text: filters, edr_locations() returns station index GeoJSON FeatureCollection, promoted sf object sf installed: Note WWDH rise-edr locations index uses _id identifier column, id. ’s fine – query verbs accept either, edr_map() auto-detect.
code: stations <- edr_locations(client, "rise-edr") nrow(stations)            # ~906 reservoirs head(stations[, c("_id", "locationName")])

title: Getting started with edr4r
text: Pick known station (Lake Mead, id 3514) parameter (3, Daily Reservoir Storage) get CoverageJSON back. Flatten tidy tibble covjson_to_tibble():
code: resp <- edr_location(   client, "rise-edr",   location_id    = 3514,   datetime       = "2023-01-01/2023-06-30",   parameter_name = "3" )  df <- covjson_to_tibble(resp) head(df) #> # A tibble: 6 × 9 #>   coverage_id parameter parameter_label              unit  datetime #>   <chr>       <chr>     <chr>                        <chr> <dttm> #> 1 1           3         Lake/Reservoir Storage       af    2023-01-01 07:00:00 #> 2 1           3         Lake/Reservoir Storage       af    2023-01-02 07:00:00 #> ...

title: Getting started with edr4r
text: edr_plot() small ggplot2 wrapper tidy tibble: Faceted parameter (different units don’t share y-axis) coloured station. Add layers themes like ggplot.
code: library(ggplot2) edr_plot(resp)            # accepts the edr_response directly

title: Getting started with edr4r
text: edr_map() puts stations leaflet basemap. data =, marker gets popup containing small inline plot “Download CSV” link station. Pass named list keyed station id ’ve fetched one CovJSON per station (server-assigned coverage_ids won’t naturally match feature id): save map standalone HTML file (embedded plots CSVs – sidecar directory):
code: data_list <- list("3514" = df) m <- edr_map(   stations[stations$`_id` == 3514, ],   data        = data_list,   id_col      = "_id",   label_col   = "locationName",   popup       = "plot+csv" ) m edr_save_html(m, "lake-mead.html")

title: Getting started with edr4r
text: quick scan small set stations, edr_explore() whole pipeline – fetch locations, fetch one time series per station, render map – single call: station gets popup. Stations server data (requested window requested parameter) fall back label attribute table.
code: edr_explore(   client, "rise-edr",   limit          = 8,                       # cap on stations to fetch   datetime       = "2023-01-01/2023-03-31",   parameter_name = "3",   file           = "rise-storage.html" )

title: Getting started with edr4r
text: datetime forgiving: pass "start/end", open interval like "2020-01-01/..", length-2 character vector. gets normalised ISO-8601 form server expects. parameter_name character vector. ’s sent one comma-separated parameter-name query parameter. monitoring networks use compound station IDs (colon-separated triplets like "1185:CO:SNTL" show snow forecast networks). work -– reserved characters get URL-encoded . literal / ID rejected, can’t survive round trip HTTP path segments matter encode . every server implements every EDR verb. locations, position, cube, area common; radius, trajectory, corridor less . client supports per spec, call collection doesn’t implement given verb returns HTTP error. edr_explore() makes one HTTP call per station. large collections, pre-filter aggressively via bbox = (server supports ) limit =. ```

what: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR)
title: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR)
text: R client service speaks OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR). spec general, practice package gets use -situ monitoring networks — stream gauges, weather stations, snow telemetry, reservoir telemetry — expose stations time series EDR. Two known-good places point : USGS waterdata OGC API — stream gauges water-quality stations U.S. Geological Survey. Western Water Datahub — pygeoapi deployment wraps RISE, SNOTEL, USACE, AWDB monitoring sources behind single EDR endpoint. goal take tedious parts EDR hands — URL construction, comma-separated parameter lists, WKT coordinate encoding, retries, content negotiation — hand back something can actually data analysis : CoverageJSON → long tibble (one row per coverage × parameter × time step), via covjson_to_tibble(). GeoJSON → sf object, via geojson_to_sf().

title: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR)
text: local development: Requires R >= 4.1. sf package optional recommended (used turn location lists GeoJSON spatial objects).
code: # from GitHub (recommended) # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("ksonda/edr4r")  # or # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("ksonda/edr4r") git clone https://github.com/ksonda/edr4r.git cd edr4r R -e 'devtools::install()'

title: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR)
text: Start pointing client server. base URL thing really needs: collection IDs (monitoring-locations, daily-values) ones used placeholders — every server advertises . first thing new service run edr_collections() read data_queries column see EDR endpoints collection supports.
code: library(edr4r)  client <- edr_client("https://api.waterdata.usgs.gov/ogcapi/beta") # or "https://api.wwdh.internetofwater.app" # or "http://localhost:5005" if you're running pygeoapi locally  edr_collections(client) #> # A tibble: N × 7 #>   id                   title                description  extent_bbox crs   data_queries links #>   <chr>                <chr>                <chr>        <list>      <chr> <list>       <list> #> 1 monitoring-locations Monitoring locations ...          <dbl [4]>   ...   <chr [3]>    ... #> 2 daily-values         Daily values         ...          <dbl [4]>   ...   <chr [3]>    ... #> ...

title: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR)
text: edr_locations() filters returns full station list GeoJSON. sf installed, gets promoted sf object automatically:
code: locs <- edr_locations(client, "monitoring-locations") locs                            # sf POINTs with station attributes plot(sf::st_geometry(locs))

what: Pull a time series for one station
title: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR)
text: know station ID, ask values. server returns CoverageJSON; covjson_to_tibble() flattens one row per (coverage × parameter × timestamp):
code: resp <- edr_location(   client, "daily-values",   location_id    = "08313000",   datetime       = "2020-01-01/2020-12-31",   parameter_name = c("discharge", "gage_height") )  df <- covjson_to_tibble(resp) df #> # A tibble: 732 × 9 #>   coverage_id parameter   parameter_label  unit  datetime                x     y     z value #>   <chr>       <chr>       <chr>            <chr> <dttm>              <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 08313000    discharge   Discharge        ft3/s 2020-01-01 00:00:00 -109.  37.0    NA   240 #> ...

title: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR)
text: grab everything inside rectangle, use edr_cube(): arbitrary polygon, edr_area() takes WKT, sf polygon, matrix (lon, lat) rows (’ll close ring ):
code: cube <- edr_cube(   client, "daily-values",   bbox           = c(-120, 39, -118, 41),   datetime       = "2023-01-01/2023-03-31",   parameter_name = "discharge" ) covjson_to_tibble(cube) ring <- matrix(   c(-109, 47, -104, 47, -104, 49, -109, 49),   ncol = 2, byrow = TRUE ) area <- edr_area(client, "monitoring-locations", coords = ring,                  datetime = "2022-01-01/..") covjson_to_tibble(area)

title: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR)
text: edr_plot() small ggplot2 wrapper tidy tibble: Facets parameter (different units don’t share y-axis) colours station. Add layers themes like ggplot.
code: edr_plot(resp)            # accepts an edr_response directly

title: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR)
text: edr_map() puts stations leaflet basemap. Pass data = named list keyed station id (shape [edr_explore()] produces) marker gets popup inline plot “Download CSV” link station’s data — embedded data: URI saved HTML selfcontained: quick exploratory pass whole collection, edr_explore() fetch + plot + map one call:
code: stations <- edr_locations(client, "monitoring-locations",                           bbox = c(-116, 35.5, -114, 36.5)) data_list <- list("3514" = covjson_to_tibble(resp)) m <- edr_map(stations, data = data_list, popup = "plot+csv") edr_save_html(m, "stations.html") edr_explore(   client, "daily-values",   bbox           = c(-116, 35.5, -114, 36.5),   datetime       = "2024-01-01/2024-03-31",   parameter_name = "discharge",   limit          = 25,   file           = "snapshot.html" )

what: Weird IDs, CSV, and an escape hatch
title: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR)
text: monitoring networks use compound station IDs — colon-separated triplets common pattern. client URL-encodes reserved characters : server advertises CSV, can ask instead CovJSON: need hit endpoint package doesn’t wrap (instances, custom queryables, anything weird), edr_request() raw escape hatch:
code: edr_location(client, "station-network", "1185:CO:SNTL",              datetime = "2024-01-01/..") edr_location(client, "daily-values", "08313000",              datetime = "2010-01-01/..", format = "csv") edr_request(client, "collections/daily-values/instances", format = "json")

title: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR)
text: server actually supports varies. Every query verb EDR spec supported client, servers implement subset. -situ monitoring deployments, locations, position, cube, area common; radius, trajectory, corridor less . Hitting verb server doesn’t implement gives HTTP error. Check data_queries column edr_collections() assume query work.

title: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR)
text: Every query verb accepts standard EDR filters: datetime — ISO-8601 instant interval. Accepts "2020-01-01/2020-12-31", open interval "2020-01-01/..", length-2 character vector c("2020-01-01", "2020-12-31"). parameter_name — character vector parameter names; sent comma-separated parameter-name= query. Use edr_queryables() discover valid names. bbox — numeric length-4 (minx, miny, maxx, maxy) length-6 (z). coords — position/area/radius/trajectory/corridor: WKT string, numeric vector / 2-column matrix lon-lat, sf/sfc geometry. z, crs, limit — passed supplied. ... — extra query parameter forwarded verbatim.

title: Client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval (EDR)
text: MIT

what: Convert a CoverageJSON response to a tidy tibble — covjson_to_tibble
title: Convert a CoverageJSON response to a tidy tibble — covjson_to_tibble
text: Flattens CoverageJSON Coverage CoverageCollection long tibble one row per (coverage, parameter, time-step). Handles Point PointSeries domain types used station-based EDR providers, falls back general N-dimensional unrolling Grid-like domains.

title: Convert a CoverageJSON response to a tidy tibble — covjson_to_tibble
code: covjson_to_tibble(x, datetime_as_posix = TRUE)

title: Convert a CoverageJSON response to a tidy tibble — covjson_to_tibble
text: x CoverageJSON object: either edr_response returned edr_location() / edr_area() / edr_cube() (etc.) format = "covjson", raw parsed list. datetime_as_posix TRUE (default), attempts parse time axis POSIXct (UTC). Falls back character failure.

title: Convert a CoverageJSON response to a tidy tibble — covjson_to_tibble
text: tibble columns coverage_id, parameter, parameter_label, unit, datetime, x, y, z, value. Columns absent source filled NA.

what: edr4r: A tidy R client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval — edr4r-package
title: edr4r: A tidy R client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval — edr4r-package
text: edr4r talks service implements OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval. general-purpose, testing real-world use date -situ monitoring networks – kind service exposes stream gauges, weather stations, snow telemetry, reservoir telemetry EDR collections.

title: edr4r: A tidy R client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval — edr4r-package
text: Two example endpoints worth pointing : USGS waterdata OGC API Western Water Datahub (pygeoapi deployment) typical session looks like: Build client edr_client(). Discover offer edr_collections() edr_queryables(). Pull data edr_locations() / edr_location(), edr_cube(), edr_area(), edr_position(), less common edr_radius() / edr_trajectory() / edr_corridor(). Flatten response covjson_to_tibble() (CoverageJSON) geojson_to_sf() (GeoJSON). everything high-level verbs cover, edr_request() raw escape hatch.

title: edr4r: A tidy R client for OGC API - Environmental Data Retrieval — edr4r-package
text: Maintainer: Kyle Onda konda@lincolninst.edu [copyright holder]

what: Area query (data inside a polygon) — edr_area
title: Area query (data inside a polygon) — edr_area
text: Calls GET /collections/{collection_id}/area WKT POLYGON coords parameter.

title: Area query (data inside a polygon) — edr_area
code: edr_area(   client,   collection_id,   coords,   datetime = NULL,   parameter_name = NULL,   z = NULL,   crs = NULL,   format = c("covjson", "json"),   ... )

title: Area query (data inside a polygon) — edr_area
text: client edr_client. collection_id Collection identifier. coords WKT polygon string, matrix / data.frame (lon, lat) rows closed POLYGON. May also sf / sfc polygon sf installed. datetime ISO-8601 instant interval, e.g. "2024-01-01/2024-12-31" "2024-01-01/..". parameter_name Character vector parameter names filter . Sent comma-separated parameter-name= query. z Vertical level filter. crs Optional CRS URI response. format "covjson" (default), "geojson", "csv", "json". ... Additional query parameters passed verbatim.

title: Create an EDR client — edr_client
text: Builds reusable client object captures base URL OGC API - EDR service, default user-agent, HTTP options applied every request.

title: Create an EDR client — edr_client
code: edr_client(   base_url,   user_agent = NULL,   timeout = 60,   max_tries = 3,   headers = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Create an EDR client — edr_client
text: base_url Base URL OGC API - EDR service. Examples: USGS waterdata OGC API "https://api.waterdata.usgs.gov/ogcapi/beta", Western Water Datahub "https://api.wwdh.internetofwater.app", "http://localhost:5005" local pygeoapi dev server. trailing slash optional. user_agent String sent User-Agent header. Defaults "edr4r/<version> (+https://github.com/ksonda/edr4r)". timeout Request timeout seconds. Defaults 60. max_tries Maximum number attempts per request. client retries 408, 429, 5xx responses exponential backoff. Defaults 3. headers Named character vector extra headers attached every request (e.g. c(Authorization = "Bearer ...")). verbose TRUE, prints request URLs console made. Useful debugging.

title: Create an EDR client — edr_client
text: object class edr_client.

title: Create an EDR client — edr_client
code: usgs <- edr_client("https://api.waterdata.usgs.gov/ogcapi/beta") usgs #> <edr_client> #>   base_url:   <https://api.waterdata.usgs.gov/ogcapi/beta> #>   user_agent: edr4r/0.1.0 (+https://github.com/ksonda/edr4r) #>   timeout:    60s #>   max_tries:  3

what: One-shot fetch + plot + map for a collection — edr_explore
title: One-shot fetch + plot + map for a collection — edr_explore
text: Convenience wrapper calls edr_locations() find stations, fetches one time series per station via edr_location(), hands lot edr_map() rendering. Optionally writes map selfcontained HTML file.

title: One-shot fetch + plot + map for a collection — edr_explore
code: edr_explore(   client,   collection_id,   bbox = NULL,   datetime = NULL,   parameter_name = NULL,   limit = NULL,   file = NULL,   popup = "plot+csv",   quiet = FALSE,   ... )

title: One-shot fetch + plot + map for a collection — edr_explore
text: client edr_client. collection_id Collection identifier. bbox Optional numeric length-4 bbox passed edr_locations(). Pre-filter collection large. datetime ISO-8601 interval forwarded edr_location(). parameter_name Character vector parameter ids; forwarded edr_location(). Use edr_parameters() discover valid ids. limit Optional cap number stations map (bbox filtering). Useful collections thousands stations. file non-NULL, write map HTML path via edr_save_html() return file invisibly. Otherwise return leaflet map. popup Popup mode (forwarded edr_map()). quiet FALSE (default), print cli progress bar fetching per-station time series. ... Forwarded edr_map().

title: One-shot fetch + plot + map for a collection — edr_explore
text: leaflet htmlwidget, invisible(file) file set.

title: One-shot fetch + plot + map for a collection — edr_explore
text: intentionally simple: one HTTP call per station. collections advertise cube area may prefer fetch stations single bbox query call edr_map() directly. Pre-filter bbox = (/limit) fetching stations want.

title: One-shot fetch + plot + map for a collection — edr_explore
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ cl <- edr_client("https://api.wwdh.internetofwater.app") edr_explore(   cl, "rise-edr",   bbox           = c(-116, 35.5, -114, 36.5),   datetime       = "2023-01-01/2023-03-31",   parameter_name = "3",   limit          = 25,   file           = tempfile(fileext = ".html") ) } # }

title: Items (OGC API Features) helpers — edr_items
text: Many EDR servers expose OGC API Features /items endpoint alongside EDR queries. Behaviour varies: deployments implement items full Features endpoint, others thin stub used register collection EDR Features. stub case, non-trivial data usually obtained via EDR queries (edr_locations(), edr_area(), edr_cube(), etc.).

title: Items (OGC API Features) helpers — edr_items
code: edr_items(   client,   collection_id,   bbox = NULL,   datetime = NULL,   limit = NULL,   format = c("geojson", "json"),   ... )  edr_item(client, collection_id, item_id, format = c("geojson", "json"), ...)

title: Items (OGC API Features) helpers — edr_items
text: client edr_client. collection_id Collection identifier. bbox Numeric vector length 4 6 (c(minx, miny, maxx, maxy) z). datetime ISO-8601 instant interval, e.g. "2024-01-01/2024-12-31" "2024-01-01/..". limit Maximum number features return. format "geojson" (default) "json". ... Additional query parameters passed verbatim. item_id Identifier single feature.

Sent comma-separated parameter-name= query. z Vertical level filter. crs Optional CRS URI response. format "covjson" (default), "geojson", "csv", "json". ... Additional query parameters passed verbatim.

Sent comma-separated parameter-name= query. crs Optional CRS URI response. limit Maximum number features return. format "geojson" (default) "json". ... Additional query parameters passed verbatim.

what: Map EDR locations with optional per-station popups — edr_map
title: Map EDR locations with optional per-station popups — edr_map
text: Builds leaflet::leaflet map station features. data supplied, marker gets popup containing small inline plot station's time series "Download CSV" link (CSV embedded data: URI resulting HTML selfcontained).

title: Map EDR locations with optional per-station popups — edr_map
code: edr_map(   locations,   data = NULL,   popup = c("plot+csv", "plot", "csv", "table", "all"),   location_col = "coverage_id",   id_col = NULL,   label_col = NULL,   parameter = NULL,   plot_width = 6,   plot_height = 3,   tile_provider = "CartoDB.Positron",   marker_radius = 6,   marker_color = "#2C7FB8" )

title: Map EDR locations with optional per-station popups — edr_map
text: locations sf object edr_locations() edr_response wrapping GeoJSON. data See . Defaults NULL. popup One "plot+csv" (default), "plot", "csv", "table", "". location_col Column data carrying location id data single tibble. Default "coverage_id". id_col Column locations join . NULL, function looks "id" "_id" first character column. label_col Column locations used popup heading. NULL, tries "name", "locationName", "title", detected id column. parameter Optional character vector restricting parameters get plotted popup. plot_width, plot_height Popup plot dimensions inches (passed underlying SVG device). Rendered ~60 px/. tile_provider Leaflet basemap. Default "CartoDB.Positron". marker_radius, marker_color Marker styling.

title: Map EDR locations with optional per-station popups — edr_map
text: leaflet htmlwidget. Pass edr_save_html() write selfcontained HTML file.

title: Map EDR locations with optional per-station popups — edr_map
text: data can one : NULL – just markers sf attribute table popup (popup = "table" popup = ""). long tibble (output covjson_to_tibble()) one column matching locations' id column. Set location_col = column data holds location id id_col = column locations. named list tibbles, keyed feature id. edr_explore() passes fetches one time series per station — right shape station CovJSON response, server-assigned coverage_ids like "1" naturally match feature id.

what: List the data parameters a collection serves — edr_parameters
title: List the data parameters a collection serves — edr_parameters
text: Pulls parameter_names block collection document (GET /collections/{id}) flattens tidy tibble. observed properties can pass parameter_name = query verbs (edr_location(), edr_cube(), etc.).

title: List the data parameters a collection serves — edr_parameters
code: edr_parameters(client, collection_id)

title: List the data parameters a collection serves — edr_parameters
text: client edr_client. collection_id Collection identifier advertised server – e.g. "monitoring-locations" "daily-values".

title: List the data parameters a collection serves — edr_parameters
text: tibble one row per parameter. Columns: id, name, description, unit_symbol, unit_label, observed_property.

title: List the data parameters a collection serves — edr_parameters
text: EDR servers vary key parameter_names dictionary (numeric IDs, short codes, etc.). id column returned tibble value pass back parameter_name; name column human-readable label.

what: Plot an EDR time-series response as a ggplot — edr_plot
title: Plot an EDR time-series response as a ggplot — edr_plot
text: Convenience wrapper around ggplot2::ggplot() long tibble returned covjson_to_tibble(). default parameter gets facet (different units share y-axis), location drawn colour.

title: Plot an EDR time-series response as a ggplot — edr_plot
code: edr_plot(   data,   parameter = NULL,   group = "coverage_id",   facet = "parameter",   scales = "free_y",   geom = c("line", "point", "both"),   facet_labels = TRUE )

title: Plot an EDR time-series response as a ggplot — edr_plot
text: data Either tidy tibble covjson_to_tibble() edr_response / edr_covjson object (flatten covjson_to_tibble() ). parameter Optional character vector restricting subset parameters. group Column data used colour aesthetic. Defaults "coverage_id" (one colour per location). Set NULL disable. facet Column facet . Defaults "parameter" variable gets panel; pass NULL plot everything one axis. scales facet_wrap() scales argument. Default "free_y" gives parameter y-axis range. geom One "line", "point", "". facet_labels TRUE (default), facet strip labels include unit (e.g. "discharge (ft3/s)").

title: Plot an EDR time-series response as a ggplot — edr_plot
text: ggplot object.

title: Plot an EDR time-series response as a ggplot — edr_plot
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ cl <- edr_client("https://api.wwdh.internetofwater.app") resp <- edr_location(cl, "rise-edr",                      location_id    = 3514,                      datetime       = "2023-01-01/2023-06-30",                      parameter_name = "3") edr_plot(resp) } # }

what: Position query (data at a point) — edr_position
title: Position query (data at a point) — edr_position
text: Calls GET /collections/{collection_id}/position WKT POINT coords parameter.

title: Position query (data at a point) — edr_position
code: edr_position(   client,   collection_id,   coords,   datetime = NULL,   parameter_name = NULL,   z = NULL,   crs = NULL,   format = c("covjson", "json"),   ... )

title: Position query (data at a point) — edr_position
text: client edr_client. collection_id Collection identifier. coords Either length-2 numeric vector c(lon, lat), length-3 vector c(lon, lat, z), WKT POINT string. datetime ISO-8601 instant interval, e.g. "2024-01-01/2024-12-31" "2024-01-01/..". parameter_name Character vector parameter names filter . Sent comma-separated parameter-name= query. z Vertical level filter. crs Optional CRS URI response. format "covjson" (default), "geojson", "csv", "json". ... Additional query parameters passed verbatim.

what: Get the queryables (filter properties) for a collection — edr_queryables
title: Get the queryables (filter properties) for a collection — edr_queryables
text: Returns OGC API queryables document collection – JSON Schema describing filter properties server exposes (typically used OGC API Features CQL2 / property-based filtering). right place look data parameters / observed properties EDR collection serves; , use edr_parameters().

title: Get the queryables (filter properties) for a collection — edr_queryables
code: edr_queryables(client, collection_id)

title: Get the queryables (filter properties) for a collection — edr_queryables
text: client edr_client. collection_id Collection identifier advertised server – e.g. "monitoring-locations" "daily-values".

title: Get the queryables (filter properties) for a collection — edr_queryables
text: list parsed queryables document.

what: Radius query (data within a radius of a point) — edr_radius
title: Radius query (data within a radius of a point) — edr_radius
text: Calls GET /collections/{collection_id}/radius.

title: Radius query (data within a radius of a point) — edr_radius
code: edr_radius(   client,   collection_id,   coords,   within,   within_units = "km",   datetime = NULL,   parameter_name = NULL,   z = NULL,   crs = NULL,   format = c("covjson", "json"),   ... )

title: Radius query (data within a radius of a point) — edr_radius
text: client edr_client. collection_id Collection identifier. coords Either length-2 numeric vector c(lon, lat), length-3 vector c(lon, lat, z), WKT POINT string. within Radius value. within_units Units within (e.g. "km", "mi"). datetime ISO-8601 instant interval, e.g. "2024-01-01/2024-12-31" "2024-01-01/..". parameter_name Character vector parameter names filter . Sent comma-separated parameter-name= query. z Vertical level filter. crs Optional CRS URI response. format "covjson" (default), "geojson", "csv", "json". ... Additional query parameters passed verbatim.

what: Perform a low-level EDR request — edr_request
title: Perform a low-level EDR request — edr_request
text: Generally need call directly: high-level verbs (edr_locations(), edr_area(), etc.) build path query string . Use edr_request() need hit bespoke path non-standard parameter.

title: Perform a low-level EDR request — edr_request
code: edr_request(   client,   path,   query = list(),   format = c("json", "geojson", "covjson", "csv", "html", "raw"),   parse = TRUE )

title: Perform a low-level EDR request — edr_request
text: client edr_client edr_client(). path Path base URL (without leading slash), e.g. "collections/monitoring-locations/locations". query Named list query parameters. Values may scalars vectors; vectors joined ",". NULL entries dropped. format Response format: one "json" (default), "geojson", "covjson", "csv", "html", "raw". Passed ?f= (except "covjson", sent ?f=json CoverageJSON Accept hint, since EDR servers return CovJSON via JSON). parse TRUE (default), parses JSON / GeoJSON / CovJSON bodies R structures. FALSE, returns raw httr2 response.

title: Perform a low-level EDR request — edr_request
text: parsed body (list / tibble / sf) httr2_response parse = FALSE.

what: Save a map to a standalone HTML file — edr_save_html
title: Save a map to a standalone HTML file — edr_save_html
text: Thin wrapper around htmlwidgets::saveWidget() leaflet map returned edr_map() edr_explore(). selfcontained = TRUE (default), embedded plot SVGs CSV download links live inside file – sidecar directory.

title: Save a map to a standalone HTML file — edr_save_html
code: edr_save_html(map, file, selfcontained = TRUE, ...)

title: Save a map to a standalone HTML file — edr_save_html
text: map leaflet htmlwidget. file Path write . selfcontained TRUE, embed assets file. ... Forwarded htmlwidgets::saveWidget().

title: Save a map to a standalone HTML file — edr_save_html
text: Invisibly returns file.

what: Trajectory query (data along a path) — edr_trajectory
title: Trajectory query (data along a path) — edr_trajectory
text: Calls GET /collections/{collection_id}/trajectory.

title: Trajectory query (data along a path) — edr_trajectory
code: edr_trajectory(   client,   collection_id,   coords,   datetime = NULL,   parameter_name = NULL,   z = NULL,   crs = NULL,   format = c("covjson", "json"),   ... )

title: Trajectory query (data along a path) — edr_trajectory
text: client edr_client. collection_id Collection identifier. coords WKT LINESTRING, matrix / data.frame (lon, lat) rows, sfc linestring. datetime ISO-8601 instant interval, e.g. "2024-01-01/2024-12-31" "2024-01-01/..". parameter_name Character vector parameter names filter . Sent comma-separated parameter-name= query. z Vertical level filter. crs Optional CRS URI response. format "covjson" (default), "geojson", "csv", "json". ... Additional query parameters passed verbatim.

what: Convert a GeoJSON EDR response to an sf object — geojson_to_sf
title: Convert a GeoJSON EDR response to an sf object — geojson_to_sf
text: Convert GeoJSON EDR response sf object

title: Convert a GeoJSON EDR response to an sf object — geojson_to_sf
code: geojson_to_sf(x)

title: Convert a GeoJSON EDR response to an sf object — geojson_to_sf
text: x edr_response wrapping GeoJSON (e.g. edr_locations()) raw parsed GeoJSON list.

title: Convert a GeoJSON EDR response to an sf object — geojson_to_sf
text: sf object. Requires sf package. sf installed, returns tibble feature properties (without geometry) warns.

title: edr4r 0.1.0
text: Initial release. Discovery: edr_landing(), edr_conformance(), edr_collections(), edr_collection(), edr_queryables(). Query verbs: edr_locations() / edr_location(), edr_items() / edr_item(), edr_position(), edr_area(), edr_cube(), edr_radius(), edr_trajectory(), edr_corridor(). Low-level escape hatch: edr_request(). Parsers: covjson_to_tibble() CoverageJSON, geojson_to_sf() GeoJSON.
